entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 20
entries.excerpt: After this Abraham went to the Philistines, and out of fear said of Sarah that she was his sister; “and Abimelech king of Gerar sent and took Sarah” (Gen 20:2). But since Sarah had already undergone temptation with Pharaoh, and moreover had Isaac in her womb, and Abraham’s prayer was unceasing, no sooner had Abimelech come up to his bed than suddenly, as upon Adam, sleep fell upon him, and God said to him in a dream: “Behold, thou art a dead man, for the woman whom thou hast taken: for she is a man’s wife.” Abimelech answered: “Wilt thou slay a righteous nation?…
